hook: Brand new vs resale landed: the data shows who blinks first

slides.sub: Brand new vs resale — the caveats show where sellers are holding firm and where they're folding.

slides.lead: Faber Hills semi-Ds are still moving — but pricing has quietly slipped.

slides.title: Three signals in the D05 data

items.body: Faber Hills semi-Ds: 6 transactions in 2024, avg S$7.26M — now printing S$6.45M–S$6.6M in 2025. Sellers moving first.
Springwood terraces (D05 RCR freehold) held S$4.09M–S$5.58M across 2024–2026, showing stickier asking floors than OCR.
Coral Island (Sentosa Cove) Feb 2025: S$14.8M on 737 sqm — PSF of S$20,081. Down from S$23,581 peak in Dec 2022.

slides.source: URA caveats · POV Guy analysis · Apr 2026

slides.title: D05 landed — latest caveat PSF
slides.source: URA caveats (land area basis) · POV Guy

slides.label: The real story
slides.punch: Faber Hills has seen consistent transaction flow through 2024–2025, but the pricing curve has bent. The Nov 2024 S$7.8M print was the local ceiling — every deal since has come in below it.
slides.punch_bold: When volume holds but price slips, that's not a crash. That's a market finding its floor.

slides.pre: Coral Island GCB median PSF — 2022 peak vs 2025
slides.post: Peak: S$23,581 psf (Sep 2022, 1,048 sqm lot). Feb 2025 print: S$20,081 psf (737 sqm). Gap widening as tenure clock ticks.
slides.source: URA caveats · Coral Island, D04 · POV Guy

quote_parts.text: third bid sets the new floor.

caption:
Resale landed sellers are repricing — quietly. Here's what the caveats show. 📉

• Faber Hills semi-Ds dropped from S$7.8M (Nov 2024) to S$6.6M (Sep 2025) — same street, -15% in 10 months
• Springwood freehold terraces in D05 RCR are holding S$5.5M–S$5.9M into Q1 2026 — stickier floors
• Coral Island GCBs (Sentosa Cove) off -15% from 2022 peak, now printing ~S$20,000 psf on land area

Volume is still there — but pricing has bent. This is a market finding its floor, not falling off a cliff.
